109	class GuidanceBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	
111	    def __init__(
112	        self,
113	        tokenizer,
114	        any_whitespace: bool = True,
115	        whitespace_pattern: Optional[str] = None,
116	        n_vocab: Optional[int] = None,
117	    ):
118	        super().__init__()
119	
120	        self.tokenizer = tokenizer
121	        self.any_whitespace = any_whitespace
122	        self.whitespace_pattern = whitespace_pattern
123	        self.llguidance_tokenizer = from_tokenizer(self.tokenizer, n_vocab)
124	
125	    def _from_serialized(self, serialized_grammar) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
126	        try:
127	            return GuidanceGrammar(
128	                llguidance_tokenizer=self.llguidance_tokenizer,
129	                serialized_grammar=serialized_grammar,
130	            )
131	        except Exception as e:
132	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid grammar: {serialized_grammar=}, {e=}")
133	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
134	
135	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
136	        try:
137	            serialized_grammar = LLMatcher.grammar_from_json_schema(
138	                key_string,
139	                defaults={
140	                    "whitespace_flexible": self.any_whitespace,
141	                    "whitespace_pattern": self.whitespace_pattern,
142	                },
143	            )
144	        except Exception as e:
145	            logger.error(f"Hit invalid json_schema: {key_string=}, {e=}")
146	            return INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ
147	        return self._from_serialized(serialized_grammar)
148	
149	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[GuidanceGrammar]:
150	        serialized_grammar = grammar_from("regex", key_string)

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import dataclasses
4	import logging
5	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple
6	
7	import torch
8	
9	import sglang.srt.sampling.penaltylib as penaltylib
10	from sglang.srt.sampling.custom_logit_processor import CustomLogitProcessor
11	from sglang.srt.sampling.sampling_params import TOP_K_ALL
12	from sglang.srt.server_args import get_global_server_args
13	
14	if TYPE_CHECKING:
15	    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import ScheduleBatch
16	
17	
18	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
19	
20	
21	@dataclasses.dataclass
22	class SamplingBatchInfo:
23	    # Basic batched sampling params
24	    temperatures: torch.Tensor
25	    top_ps: torch.Tensor
26	    top_ks: torch.Tensor
27	    min_ps: torch.Tensor
28	
29	    # Whether all requests use greedy sampling
30	    is_all_greedy: bool
31	
32	    # Whether any requests use top_p sampling
33	    need_top_p_sampling: bool
34	
35	    # Whether any requests use top_k sampling
36	    need_top_k_sampling: bool
37	
38	    # Whether any request needs min_p sampling
39	    need_min_p_sampling: bool
40	
41	    # Masking tensors for grammar-guided structured outputs
42	    vocab_size: int
43	    grammars: Optional[List] = None
44	    vocab_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
45	    apply_mask_func: Optional[Callable[[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor], None]] = None
46	
47	    # Penalizer
48	    penalizer_orchestrator: Optional[penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator] = None
49	    acc_linear_penalties: torch.Tensor = None  # Used in the overlap mode
50	
51	    # Whether any request has custom logit processor
52	    has_custom_logit_processor: bool = False
53	    # Custom parameters
54	    custom_params: Optional[List[Optional[Dict[str, Any]]]] = None
55	    # Custom logit processor
56	    custom_logit_processor: Optional[
57	        Dict[int, Tuple[CustomLogitProcessor, torch.Tensor]]
58	    ] = None
59	
60	    # Used for deterministic sampling
61	    sampling_seed: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
62	
63	    # Device
64	    device: str = "cuda"
65	
66	    # Handle logit bias
67	    logit_bias: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
68	
69	    @classmethod
70	    def from_schedule_batch(cls, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
71	        global_server_args = get_global_server_args()
72	        enable_deterministic = global_server_args.enable_deterministic_inference
73	
74	        reqs = batch.reqs
75	        device = batch.device
76	        temperatures = torch.tensor(
77	            [r.sampling_params.temperature for r in reqs],
78	            dtype=torch.float,
79	            device=device,
80	        ).view(-1, 1)
81	        top_ps = torch.tensor(
82	            [r.sampling_params.top_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
83	        )
84	        top_ks = torch.tensor(
85	            [r.sampling_params.top_k for r in reqs], dtype=torch.int32, device=device
86	        )
87	        min_ps = torch.tensor(
88	            [r.sampling_params.min_p for r in reqs], dtype=torch.float, device=device
89	        )
90	        sampling_seed = (
91	            torch.tensor(
92	                [
93	                    (
94	                        r.sampling_params.sampling_seed
95	                        if r.sampling_params.sampling_seed is not None
96	                        else 42
97	                    )
98	                    for r in reqs
99	                ],
100	                dtype=torch.int64,
101	                device=device,
102	            )
103	            if enable_deterministic
104	            else None
105	        )
106	
107	        logit_bias = None
108	        if any(r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None for r in reqs):
109	            logit_bias = torch.zeros(len(reqs), vocab_size, device=device)
110	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
111	                if r.sampling_params.logit_bias is not None:
112	                    for key, value in r.sampling_params.logit_bias.items():
113	                        logit_bias[i, int(key)] = value
114	
115	        # Check if any request has custom logit processor
116	        has_custom_logit_processor = (
117	            global_server_args.enable_custom_logit_processor
118	            and any(r.custom_logit_processor for r in reqs)  # check the flag first.
119	        )  # then check the requests.
120	
121	        if has_custom_logit_processor:
122	            # Merge the same type of custom logit processors together
123	            processor_dict = {}
124	            for i, r in enumerate(reqs):
125	                if r.custom_logit_processor is None:
126	                    continue
127	                processor_str = r.custom_logit_processor
128	                if processor_str not in processor_dict:
129	                    processor_dict[processor_str] = []
130	                processor_dict[processor_str].append(i)
131	
132	            merged_custom_logit_processor = {
133	                hash(processor_str): (
134	                    # The deserialized custom logit processor object
135	                    CustomLogitProcessor.from_str(processor_str),
136	                    # The mask tensor for the requests that use this custom logit processor
137	                    torch.zeros(len(reqs), dtype=torch.bool)
138	                    .scatter_(0, torch.tensor(true_indices), True)
139	                    .to(device, non_blocking=True),
140	                )
141	                for processor_str, true_indices in processor_dict.items()
142	            }
143	            custom_params = [r.sampling_params.custom_params for r in reqs]
144	        else:
145	            merged_custom_logit_processor = None
146	            custom_params = None
147	
148	        # Each penalizers will do nothing if they evaluate themselves as not required by looking at
149	        # the sampling_params of the requests (See {_is_required()} of each penalizers). So this
150	        # should not add hefty computation overhead other than simple checks.
151	        #
152	        # While we can choose not to even create the class instances if they are not required, this
153	        # could add additional complexity to the {ScheduleBatch} class, especially we need to
154	        # handle {filter_batch()} and {merge_batch()} cases as well.
155	        penalizer_orchestrator = penaltylib.BatchedPenalizerOrchestrator(
156	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
157	            batch=batch,
158	            penalizers={
159	                penaltylib.BatchedFrequencyPenalizer,
160	                penaltylib.BatchedMinNewTokensPenalizer,
161	                penaltylib.BatchedPresencePenalizer,
162	            },
163	        )
164	
165	        ret = cls(
166	            temperatures=temperatures,
167	            top_ps=top_ps,
168	            top_ks=top_ks,
169	            min_ps=min_ps,
170	            sampling_seed=sampling_seed,
171	            is_all_greedy=all(r.sampling_params.top_k <= 1 for r in reqs),
172	            need_top_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_p != 1.0 for r in reqs),
173	            need_top_k_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.top_k != TOP_K_ALL for r in reqs),
174	            need_min_p_sampling=any(r.sampling_params.min_p > 0 for r in reqs),
175	            vocab_size=vocab_size,
176	            penalizer_orchestrator=penalizer_orchestrator,
177	            has_custom_logit_processor=has_custom_logit_processor,
178	            custom_params=custom_params,
179	            custom_logit_processor=merged_custom_logit_processor,
180	            device=device,
181	            logit_bias=logit_bias,
182	        )
183	        ret.adjusted_from_schedule_batch(batch, vocab_size)
184	        return ret
185	
186	    # placeholder for override
187	    def adjusted_from_schedule_batch(self, batch: ScheduleBatch, vocab_size: int):
188	        pass
189	
190	    # placeholder for override
191	    def adjusted_merge_batch(self, other: "SamplingBatchInfo"):
192	        pass
193	
194	    def __len__(self):
195	        return len(self.temperatures)
196	
197	    def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
198	        if not self.grammars:
199	            self.vocab_mask = None
200	            self.apply_mask_func = None

5	    # test whether an object is a legacy structural tag

27	class ReasonerGrammarObject(BaseGrammarObject):
28	    def __init__(self, grammar: BaseGrammarObject, think_end_id: int):
29	        super().__init__()
30	        self.grammar = grammar
31	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id
32	        # -1    means thinking has not ended yet
33	        # 0     means just ended thinking in the last token
34	        # +     means number of tokens after thinking ended
35	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
36	
37	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
38	        self.tokens_after_think_end = -1 if reasoning else 0
39	
40	    def transfer_state(self, token: int) -> int:
41	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == -1 and token == self.think_end_id:
42	            self.tokens_after_think_end = 0
43	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
44	            self.tokens_after_think_end += 1
45	
46	    def rollback_state(self):
47	        if self.tokens_after_think_end == 0:
48	            self.tokens_after_think_end = -1
49	        elif self.tokens_after_think_end > 0:
50	            self.tokens_after_think_end -= 1
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int):
53	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
54	            self.grammar.accept_token(token)
55	        self.transfer_state(token)
56	
57	    def is_terminated(self):
58	        return self.grammar.is_terminated()
59	
60	    def rollback(self, k):
61	        steps_after_think = min(k, self.tokens_after_think_end)
62	        if steps_after_think > 0:
63	            self.grammar.rollback(steps_after_think)
64	
65	        for _ in range(k):
66	            self.rollback_state()
67

69	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
70	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
71	        return self.grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(vocab_size, batch_size, device)
72	
73	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
74	        if self.tokens_after_think_end >= 0:
75	            self.grammar.fill_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, idx)
76	
77	    def move_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
78	        return self.grammar.move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask, device)
79	
80	    @property
81	    def apply_vocab_mask(self):
82	        return self.grammar.apply_vocab_mask

84	    def copy(self) -> BaseGrammarObject:
85	        return ReasonerGrammarObject(self.grammar.copy(), self.think_end_id)
86	
87	    @property
88	    def finished(self):
89	        return self.grammar.finished
90	
91	    @finished.setter
92	    def finished(self, finished):
93	        self.grammar.finished = finished
94	
95	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer):
96	        return self.grammar.try_jump_forward(tokenizer)
97	
98	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper):

101	    def jump_and_retokenize(
102	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
103	    ):

109	class ReasonerGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
110	    def __init__(self, grammar_backend: BaseGrammarBackend, think_end_id):
111	        super().__init__()
112	        self.grammar_backend = grammar_backend
113	        self.think_end_id = think_end_id

115	    def _init_value_dispatch(
116	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], reasoning: bool
117	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
118	        ret = self.grammar_backend._init_value_dispatch(key, reasoning)
119	        # avoid wrapping invalid grammar, so that the scheduler can detect it
120	        if ret is None or ret is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
121	            return ret
122	        obj = ReasonerGrammarObject(ret, self.think_end_id)
123	        obj.maybe_init_reasoning(reasoning)
124	        return obj

## Summary: SGLang Frontend API for Output Constraints

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here's a complete understanding of how the frontend API handles output constraints:

### 1. **User-Facing gen() API**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775472433150-0u2jyw/python/sglang/lang/api.py` (lines 75-139)

The `gen()` function accepts constraint parameters:
```python
def gen(
    name: Optional[str] = None,
    max_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    min_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
    n: Optional[int] = None,
    stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
```

```python
def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
    if (
        req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
        or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
        or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
        or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
    ):
        if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
            key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
        elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
            key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
        elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
            key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
        elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
            key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
        
        value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
            key, req.require_reasoning
        )
        req.grammar = value
```

## SGLang Output Constraint System - Comprehensive Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here is a detailed understanding of the output constraint system:

---

### 1. WHERE EXISTING OUTPUT CONSTRAINTS ARE DEFINED

**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`

**Key Class:** `SamplingParams`

The constraints are defined as optional string parameters in the `__init__` method:
- `json_schema: Optional[str] = None` - JSON Schema constraint
- `regex: Optional[str] = None` - Regular expression constraint
- `ebnf: Optional[str] = None` - EBNF grammar constraint
- `structural_tag: Optional[str] = None` - Structural tag constraint

These are mutually exclusive (validation at line 155-161):
```python
grammars = [self.json_schema, self.regex, self.ebnf]
if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
    raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
```

**Additional related files:**
- `/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` - OpenAI API protocol definitions (lines 268-270, 553-584)
- `/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py` - JSON schema utilities for tool calling

---

### 2. HOW CONSTRAINTS ARE REGISTERED/DISPATCHED

**Main Registry System:**

**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` (lines 199-274)

The dispatch system uses a registry pattern:
```python
GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}

def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func

def create_grammar_backend(server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids=None):
    # Looks up backend by name
    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](...)
```

**Dispatch Method Structure (lines 157-179):**
```python
def _init_value_dispatch(self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool):
    key_type, key_string = key
    if key_type == "json":
        grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
    elif key_type == "regex":
        grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
    elif key_type == "ebnf":
        grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
    elif key_type == "structural_tag":
        grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
    # ... more types ...
```

**Backend Types (lines 221-273):**
- `"outlines"` - Uses Outlines library
- `"xgrammar"` - Uses XGrammar library (default)
- `"llguidance"` - Uses LLGuidance library
- `"none"` - No constraints enabled

---

### 3. STRUCTURE OF A CONSTRAINT CLASS/FUNCTION

**Base Class:** `BaseGrammarObject` (lines 42-118)

**Required Methods:**

```python
class BaseGrammarObject:
    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
        """Accept a token in the grammar."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def allocate_vocab_mask(self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Allocate vocabulary mask tensor for batch."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
        """Fill vocab mask for this request's constraints."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    @staticmethod
    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
        """Move vocab mask to device."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    @staticmethod
    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
        """Apply vocab mask to logits."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject":
        """Create a copy of this grammar object."""
        return self
    
    def is_terminated(self) -> bool:
        """Check if grammar has terminated."""
        return False
    
    def rollback(self, k: int):
        """Rollback k tokens."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
        """Attempt to jump forward in the grammar."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
        """Jump forward and update state."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def jump_and_retokenize(self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int) -> None:
        """Handle jump forward with retokenization."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
```

**Backend Base Class:** `BaseGrammarBackend` (lines 129-197)

Required dispatch methods:
```python
class BaseGrammarBackend:
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
    
    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
```

---

### 4. HOW CONSTRAINTS ARE USED IN SGLANG PROGRAMS (USER-FACING API)

**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`

**Native SGLang API:**
```python
sampling_params = SamplingParams(
    max_new_tokens=128,
    json_schema=json.dumps(schema_dict),  # or
    regex=r"^\d{3}$",  # or
    ebnf='root ::= "Hello" | "Hi"',  # or
    structural_tag=tag_dict
)
```

**OpenAI-Compatible API:** `/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py`

```python
# CompletionRequest (lines 260-280)
request = CompletionRequest(
    prompt="...",
    json_schema=schema_string,
    regex=pattern,
    ebnf=grammar,
    response_format={
        "type": "json_schema",
        "json_schema": {
            "name": "response",
            "schema": {...}
        }
    }
)

# ChatCompletionRequest (lines 550-584)
request = ChatCompletionRequest(
    messages=[...],
    response_format={
        "type": "json_schema",
        "json_schema": {...}
    }
)
```

**Tool Calling with JSON Schema:** `/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py`

```python
def get_json_schema_constraint(tools: List[Tool], tool_choice) -> Optional[dict]:
    """Generate JSON schema constraint from tool definitions."""
    # Returns JSON schema for constrained tool calling
```

---

### 5. RELEVANT TEST FILES FOR CONSTRAINTS

**Primary Test Files:**
- `/test/registered/constrained_decoding/test_constrained_decoding.py` - Main constraint tests
- `/test/registered/function_call/test_json_schema_constraint.py` - JSON schema for tools
- `/test/registered/spec/test_constrained_decoding_spec_reasoning.py` - Speculative decoding with constraints

**Test Mixin Classes:** `/python/sglang/test/kits/`
- `json_constrained_kit.py` - TestJSONConstrainedMixin
- `regex_constrained_kit.py` - TestRegexConstrainedMixin
- `ebnf_constrained_kit.py` - TestEBNFConstrainedMixin
- `matched_stop_kit.py` - Stop string matching

**Backend-Specific Test Classes:**
- `TestXGrammarBackend` - Tests all constraint types with xgrammar
- `TestOutlinesBackend` - Tests JSON constraints with outlines
- `TestLLGuidanceBackend` - Tests all constraint types with llguidance

---

### 6. WHERE CONSTRAINT VALIDATION/ENFORCEMENT HAPPENS (BACKEND GENERATION)

**Constraint Processing Pipeline:**

**Step 1: Grammar Manager** - `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py`

```python
class GrammarManager:
    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
        if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
            key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
        elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
            key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
        elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
            key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
        elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
            key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
        
        value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(key)
        req.grammar = value
```

**Step 2: Backend Compilation** - Each backend (xgrammar_backend.py, outlines_backend.py, llguidance_backend.py)

**XGrammar Backend** (`/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py`):
```python
class XGrammarGrammarBackend(BaseGrammarBackend):
    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_json_schema(schema=key_string)
        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="json"))
    
    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_regex(key_string)
        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="regex"))
    
    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[XGrammarGrammar]:
        ctx = self.grammar_compiler.compile_grammar(key_string)
        return self._from_context(ctx, key_string, GrammarStats(dispatch_type="ebnf"))
```

**XGrammar Grammar Object** (`/python/sglang/srt/constrained/xgrammar_backend.py`, lines 53-167):
```python
class XGrammarGrammar(BaseGrammarObject):
    def __init__(self, matcher: GrammarMatcher, vocab_size, ctx, override_stop_tokens):
        self.matcher = matcher  # XGrammar's token matcher
    
    def accept_token(self, token: int):
        accepted = self.matcher.accept_token(token)
    
    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
        self.matcher.fill_next_token_bitmask(vocab_mask, idx)
    
    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
        # Uses Triton ops to apply bitmask to logits
        apply_token_bitmask_inplace_triton(logits, vocab_mask)
```

**Step 3: Sampling with Constraints** - `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_batch_info.py`

```python
@dataclasses.dataclass
class SamplingBatchInfo:
    grammars: Optional[List] = None
    vocab_mask: Optional[torch.Tensor] = None
    apply_mask_func: Optional[Callable[[torch.Tensor, torch.Tensor], None]] = None

def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):
    if not self.grammars:
        self.vocab_mask = None
        return
    
    first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.allocate_vocab_mask(self.vocab_size, len(self.grammars), self.device)
    
    self.apply_mask_func = first_grammar.apply_vocab_mask
    
    for i, grammar in enumerate(self.grammars):
        if grammar and not grammar.finished and not grammar.is_terminated():
            grammar.fill_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, i)
    
    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)

def apply_logits_mask(self, logits):
    if self.vocab_mask is not None:
        self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
```

**Step 4: Token Acceptance** - `/python/sglang/srt/managers/schedule_batch.py`

```python
class Req:
    grammar: Optional[BaseGrammarObject] = None
    
    def append_output_ids(self, output_ids):
        # After token generation, accept it in the grammar
        if self.grammar is not None:
            if self.grammar.is_terminated():
                self.set_finish()
            else:
                self.grammar.accept_token(output_ids[-1])
```

---

### 7. ARCHITECTURE SUMMARY

**Complete Data Flow:**

```
User Request
    ↓
SamplingParams(json_schema/regex/ebnf/structural_tag)
    ↓
GrammarManager.process_req_with_grammar()
    ├─ Key = (constraint_type, constraint_string)
    ├─ Cache lookup or async compilation
    └─ req.grammar = BaseGrammarObject
    ↓
ScheduleBatch creates SamplingBatchInfo
    ├─ Collects all grammars from requests
    └─ update_regex_vocab_mask()
    ↓
During Sampling (sampler.py)
    ├─ Generate logits
    ├─ Apply vocab_mask (fill_vocab_mask → apply_vocab_mask)
    ├─ Sample tokens
    └─ Apply other modifiers (temperature, top-p, etc)
    ↓
Token Acceptance
    ├─ grammar.accept_token(token_id)
    ├─ Check if grammar terminated
    └─ Rollback if needed (for speculative decoding)
```

|-----------|-----------|----------------------|
| **Constraints Definition** | `/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` | `SamplingParams` |

| **Outlines Backend** | `/srt/constrained/outlines_backend.py` | `OutlinesGrammarBackend`, `OutlinesGrammar` |

| **Tests** | `/test/registered/constrained_decoding/` | Test mixins & backends |

---

### 9. CACHE SYSTEM FOR CONSTRAINTS

**Location:** `BaseGrammarBackend` (lines 181-196)

```python
class BaseGrammarBackend:
    def __init__(self):
        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()  # Async compilation
        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}

def get_cached_or_future_value(self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool):
    value = self.cache.get(key)
    if value:
        copied_value = value.copy()  # Clone for each request
        copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
        return copied_value, True  # Cache hit
    
    value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
    return value, False  # Cache miss, async compilation
```

Cache key format: `(constraint_type, constraint_string)` where constraint_type is one of: `"json"`, `"regex"`, `"ebnf"`, `"structural_tag"`, `"structural_pattern"`, `"structural_pattern_v2"`.

## SGLang Output Constraint System - Comprehensive Analysis

Based on my thorough exploration of the SGLang codebase, here is a detailed understanding of the output constraint system:

---

### 1. WHERE EXISTING OUTPUT CONSTRAINTS ARE DEFINED

**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py`

**Key Class:** `SamplingParams`

The constraints are defined as optional string parameters in the `__init__` method:
- `json_schema: Optional[str] = None` - JSON Schema constraint
- `regex: Optional[str] = None` - Regular expression constraint
- `ebnf: Optional[str] = None` - EBNF grammar constraint
- `structural_tag: Optional[str] = None` - Structural tag constraint

These are mutually exclusive (validation at line 155-161):
```python
grammars = [self.json_schema, self.regex, self.ebnf]
if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
    raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")
```

**Additional related files:**
- `/python/sglang/srt/entrypoints/openai/protocol.py` - OpenAI API protocol definitions (lines 268-270, 553-584)
- `/python/sglang/srt/function_call/utils.py` - JSON schema utilities for tool calling

---

### 2. HOW CONSTRAINTS ARE REGISTERED/DISPATCHED

**Main Registry System:**

**File:** `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` (lines 199-274)

The dispatch system uses a registry pattern:
```python
GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}

def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func

def create_grammar_backend(server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids=None):
    # Looks up backend by name
    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](...)
```

**Dispatch Method Structure (lines 157-179):**
```python
def _init_value_dispatch(self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool):
    key_type, key_string = key
    if key_type == "json":
        grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
    elif key_type == "regex":
        grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
    elif key_type == "ebnf":
        grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
    elif key_type == "structural_tag":
        grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
    # ... more types ...
```

**Backend Types (lines 221-273):**
- `"outlines"` - Uses Outlines library
- `"xgrammar"` - Uses XGrammar library (default)
- `"llguidance"` - Uses LLGuidance library
- `"none"` - No constraints enabled

---

### 3. STRUCTURE OF A CONSTRAINT CLASS/FUNCTION

**Base Class:** `BaseGrammarObject` (lines 42-118)

**Required Methods:**

```python
        """Fill vocab mask for this request's constraints."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    @staticmethod

        """Move vocab mask to device."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    @staticmethod
    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
        """Apply vocab mask to logits."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject":
        """Create a copy of this grammar object."""
        return self
    
    def is_terminated(self) -> bool:
        """Check if grammar has terminated."""
        return False
    
    def rollback(self, k: int):

        raise NotImplementedError()

        """Attempt to jump forward in the grammar."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
        """Jump forward and update state."""
        raise NotImplementedError()
    
    def jump_and_retokenize(self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int) -> None:
```

```python

def update_regex_vocab_mask(self):

    first_grammar = next(grammar for grammar in self.grammars if grammar)

    self.vocab_mask = first_grammar.move_vocab_mask(self.vocab_mask, self.device)

def apply_logits_mask(self, logits):

        self.apply_mask_func(logits=logits, vocab_mask=self.vocab_mask)
```

Now I have a comprehensive understanding of the SGLang codebase. Let me now read the key files directly to get the exact details I need for planning, then design the implementation.

Let me read the critical files to understand exact line numbers, code patterns, and how I should integrate `json_path`.

Key files to read:
1. `/python/sglang/srt/sampling/sampling_params.py` - Where to add `json_path` parameter
2. `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/grammar_manager.py` - Where to add routing for `json_path`


3. `/python/sglang/srt/constrained/base_grammar_backend.py` - Where to add `dispatch_json_path`
4. `/python/sglang/lang/ir.py` - Frontend IR
5. `/python/sglang/lang/api.py` - Frontend API

Now I'm thinking through what a JSON path constraint actually means in the context of constrained generation. The model needs to produce valid JSON output where the specified JSONPath expression matches at least one value in the generated output. But this is about constraining token generation in real-time, not post-processing validation.

I see a few viable approaches: I could validate the JSON after generation, but that doesn't actually constrain the tokens being produced. I could convert the JSONPath into a JSON schema that describes the required structure, then leverage the existing `json_schema` constraint system. Or I could derive an EBNF grammar from the JSONPath. The most practical solution would be converting the JSONPath to a JSON schema and reusing the existing infrastructure, though I could also add a dedicated `json_path` constraint type if needed.

Actually, I'm realizing that a JSONPath like `$.store.book[*].author` is fundamentally a query expression, not a structural template. For generation purposes, the meaningful interpretation is: "produce JSON that contains the nested structure this path implies." So my implementation strategy would be adding `json_path` as a first-class parameter, creating a utility to convert JSONPath expressions into equivalent JSON schemas, then having each backend's dispatcher transform the path to a schema and delegate to the existing JSON dispatch logic.

14	"""The baseclass of a backend for grammar-guided constrained decoding."""
15	
16	import logging
17	import time
18	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor
19	from dataclasses import dataclass, field

30	@dataclass
31	class GrammarStats:
32	    compilation_time: Optional[float] = None
33	    schema_count: Optional[int] = None
34	    ebnf_size: Optional[int] = None
35	    is_cache_hit: bool = False
36	    is_grammar_aborted: bool = False
37	    tree_traversal_time: List[float] = field(default_factory=list)
38	    dispatch_type: Optional[str] = None
39	    num_timeout: int = 0

42	class BaseGrammarObject:
43	
44	    def __init__(self):
45	        self._finished = False
46	        self.grammar_stats = None
47	        self.current_token = None
48	
49	    def maybe_init_reasoning(self, reasoning: bool):
50	        pass
51	
52	    def accept_token(self, token: int) -> None:
53	        """
54	        Accept a token in the grammar.
55	        """
56	        raise NotImplementedError()
57	
58	    def rollback(self, k: int):
59	        raise NotImplementedError()
60	
61	    def is_terminated(self):
62	        return False
63	
64	    def allocate_vocab_mask(
65	        self, vocab_size: int, batch_size: int, device
66	    ) -> torch.Tensor:
67	        raise NotImplementedError()
68	
69	    def fill_vocab_mask(self, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, idx: int) -> None:
70	        raise NotImplementedError()
71	
72	    @staticmethod
73	    def move_vocab_mask(vocab_mask: torch.Tensor, device) -> torch.Tensor:
74	        raise NotImplementedError()
75	
76	    @staticmethod
77	    def apply_vocab_mask(logits: torch.Tensor, vocab_mask: torch.Tensor) -> None:
78	        raise NotImplementedError()
79	
80	    def copy(self) -> "BaseGrammarObject":
81	        return self
82	
83	    @property
84	    def finished(self):
85	        return self._finished
86	
87	    @finished.setter
88	    def finished(self, finished):
89	        self._finished = finished
90	
91	    def try_jump_forward(self, tokenizer) -> Optional[Tuple[List[int], str]]:
92	        """
93	        Try to jump forward in the grammar.
94	
95	        Returns:
96	            A jump forward helper which may be used in `jump_forward_str_state`.
97	            None if the jump forward is not possible.
98	        """
99	        raise NotImplementedError()
100	
101	    def jump_forward_str_state(self, helper: Tuple[List[int], str]) -> Tuple[str, int]:
102	        """
103	        Jump forward for the grammar.
104	
105	        Returns:
106	            A tuple of the jump forward string and the next state of the grammar
107	            (which can be used in `jump_and_retokenize` if needed).
108	        """
109	        raise NotImplementedError()
110	
111	    def jump_and_retokenize(
112	        self, old_output_ids: List[int], new_output_ids: List[int], next_state: int
113	    ) -> None:

116	        """
117	        raise NotImplementedError()

123	@dataclass
124	class CacheEntry:
125	    value: BaseGrammarObject
126	    event: Event
127	
128	
129	class BaseGrammarBackend:
130	    def __init__(self):
131	        self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor()
132	        self.cache: Dict[Tuple[str, str], CacheEntry] = {}
133	
134	    def _not_supported(self, key_type: str, key_string: str) -> None:
135	        logger.warning(f"Skip unsupported {key_type=}, {key_string=}")
136	
137	    def dispatch_fallback(
138	        self, key_type: str, key_string: str
139	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
140	        """
141	        This function should not be reached in any case.
142	        """
143	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid key_type: {key_type}={key_string}")
144	
145	    def dispatch_json(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
146	        return self._not_supported("json", key_string)
147	
148	    def dispatch_regex(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
149	        return self._not_supported("regex", key_string)
150	
151	    def dispatch_ebnf(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
152	        return self._not_supported("ebnf", key_string)
153	
154	    def dispatch_structural_tag(self, key_string: str) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
155	        return self._not_supported("structural_tag", key_string)
156	
157	    def _init_value_dispatch(
158	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
159	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
160	        s = time.perf_counter()
161	        key_type, key_string = key
162	        if key_type == "json":
163	            grammar = self.dispatch_json(key_string)
164	        elif key_type == "regex":
165	            grammar = self.dispatch_regex(key_string)
166	        elif key_type == "ebnf":
167	            grammar = self.dispatch_ebnf(key_string)
168	        elif key_type == "structural_tag":
169	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_tag(key_string)
170	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern":
171	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern(key_string)
172	        elif key_type == "structural_pattern_v2":
173	            grammar = self.dispatch_structural_pattern_v2(key_string)
174	        else:
175	            grammar = self.dispatch_fallback(key_type, key_string)
176	
177	        if grammar is not None and grammar.grammar_stats is not None:
178	            grammar.grammar_stats.compilation_time = time.perf_counter() - s
179	        return grammar
180	
181	    def get_cached_or_future_value(
182	        self, key: Tuple[str, str], require_reasoning: bool
183	    ) -> Optional[BaseGrammarObject]:
184	        value = self.cache.get(key)
185	        if value:
186	            copied_value = value.copy()
187	            copied_value.maybe_init_reasoning(require_reasoning)
188	            return copied_value, True
189	        value = self.executor.submit(self._init_value_dispatch, key, require_reasoning)
190	        return value, False
191	
192	    def set_cache(self, key: Tuple[str, str], value: BaseGrammarObject):
193	        self.cache[key] = value
194	
195	    def reset(self):
196	        self.cache.clear()
197	
198	
199	GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY = {}
200	
201	
202	def register_grammar_backend(name, init_func):
203	    GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name] = init_func
204	
205	
206	def create_grammar_backend(
207	    server_args: ServerArgs,
208	    tokenizer,
209	    vocab_size: int,
210	    eos_token_ids: Optional[set] = None,
211	) -> Optional[BaseGrammarBackend]:
212	    name = server_args.grammar_backend
213	
214	    # Custom grammar backend has the highest priority
215	    if name in GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY:
216	        return GRAMMAR_BACKEND_REGISTRY[name](
217	            server_args, tokenizer, vocab_size, eos_token_ids
218	        )
219	
220	    # Default grammar backends
221	    if name == "outlines":
222	        from sglang.srt.constrained.outlines_backend import OutlinesGrammarBackend
223	
224	        grammar_backend = OutlinesGrammarBackend(
225	            tokenizer,
226	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
227	        )
228	    elif name == "xgrammar":
229	        from sglang.srt.constrained.xgrammar_backend import (
230	            TokenizerNotSupportedError,
231	            XGrammarGrammarBackend,
232	        )
233	
234	        # Convert Set[int] to List[int] if needed
235	        eos_list = list(eos_token_ids) if eos_token_ids else None
236	
237	        try:
238	            grammar_backend = XGrammarGrammarBackend(
239	                tokenizer,
240	                vocab_size=vocab_size,
241	                model_eos_token_ids=eos_list,
242	                any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
243	            )
244	        except TokenizerNotSupportedError as e:
245	            logger.warning(
246	                f"Grammar backend disabled because tokenizer is not supported by XGrammar: {e}. "
247	                "Falling back to grammar_backend='none'. "
248	                "Structured outputs (JSON schema, regex, EBNF) will not be available."
249	            )
250	            server_args.grammar_backend = "none"
251	            return None
252	    elif name == "llguidance":
253	        from sglang.srt.constrained.llguidance_backend import GuidanceBackend
254	
255	        grammar_backend = GuidanceBackend(
256	            tokenizer=tokenizer,
257	            any_whitespace=not server_args.constrained_json_disable_any_whitespace,
258	            whitespace_pattern=server_args.constrained_json_whitespace_pattern,
259	        )
260	    elif name == "none":
261	        return None
262	    else:
263	        raise ValueError(f"Invalid grammar backend: {name}")
264	
265	    if server_args.reasoning_parser and hasattr(tokenizer, "think_end_id"):
266	        from sglang.srt.constrained.reasoner_grammar_backend import (
267	            ReasonerGrammarBackend,
268	        )
269	
270	        grammar_backend = ReasonerGrammarBackend(
271	            grammar_backend, tokenizer.think_end_id
272	        )
273	
274	    return grammar_backend
275

1	from __future__ import annotations
2	
3	import logging
4	import time
5	from concurrent import futures
6	from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List
7	
8	import torch
9	
10	from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
11	    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
12	    create_grammar_backend,
13	)
14	from sglang.srt.environ import envs
15	
16	if TYPE_CHECKING:
17	    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
18	    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
19	    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler
20	
21	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
22	
23	
24	class GrammarManager:
25	    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
26	        self.scheduler = scheduler
27	        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
28	        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
29	        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
30	            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
31	                self.server_args,
32	                scheduler.tokenizer,
33	                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
34	                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
35	            )
36	        else:
37	            self.grammar_backend = None
38	
39	        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
40	        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
41	        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
42	        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank
43	
44	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
45	        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
46	            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
47	        )
48	
49	    def __len__(self):
50	        return len(self.grammar_queue)
51	
52	    def clear(self):
53	        if self.grammar_backend:
54	            self.grammar_backend.reset()
55	
56	    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
57	        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0
58	
59	    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
60	        for req in self.grammar_queue:
61	            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
62	                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
63	                if req.grammar:
64	                    req.grammar.cancel()
65	                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")
66	
67	    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
68	        # Init grammar cache for this request
69	        add_to_grammar_queue = False
70	        if (
71	            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
72	            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
73	            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
74	            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
75	        ):
76	            if self.grammar_backend is None:
77	                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
78	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
79	            else:
80	                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
81	                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
82	                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
83	                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
84	                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
85	                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
86	                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
87	                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)
88	
89	                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
90	                    key, req.require_reasoning
91	                )
92	                req.grammar = value
93	
94	                if not cache_hit:
95	                    req.grammar_key = key
96	                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
97	                else:
98	                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
99	                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
100	                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
101	
102	        if add_to_grammar_queue:
103	            self.grammar_queue.append(req)
104	
105	        return add_to_grammar_queue
106	
107	    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
108	        """
109	        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.
110	
111	        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
112	        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
113	        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)
114	
115	        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
116	        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
117	        """
118	        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
119	        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
120	
121	        # Poll for ready requests
122	        start_time = time.perf_counter()
123	        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
124	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
125	                if i in ready_req_idxs:
126	                    continue
127	
128	                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
129	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
130	                    continue
131	
132	                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
133	                if req.grammar.done():
134	                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
135	
136	            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
137	            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)
138	
139	        # Check failed requests
140	        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
141	            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
142	                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
143	                if (
144	                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
145	                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
146	                ):
147	                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
148	                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
149	                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)
150	
151	        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
152	        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
153	            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
154	            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
155	        else:
156	            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
157	            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
158	                all_gather_output,
159	                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
160	                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
161	            )
162	            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
163	            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])
164	
165	        # Return ready requests
166	        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
167	        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
168	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
169	            return_reqs.append(req)
170	            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
171	                continue
172	
173	            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
174	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
175	            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
176	                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
177	                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
178	
179	        # Return failed requests
180	        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
181	            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
182	            return_reqs.append(req)
183	
184	            req.grammar.cancel()
185	            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
186	            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
187	            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
188	
189	        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
190	        self.grammar_queue = [
191	            req
192	            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
193	            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
194	        ]
195	        return return_reqs
196

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import time
from concurrent import futures
from typing import TYPE_CHECKING, List

import torch

from sglang.srt.constrained.base_grammar_backend import (
    INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ,
    create_grammar_backend,
)
from sglang.srt.environ import envs

if TYPE_CHECKING:
    from sglang.srt.managers.io_struct import AbortReq
    from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import Req
    from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class GrammarManager:
    def __init__(self, scheduler: Scheduler):
        self.scheduler = scheduler
        self.server_args = scheduler.server_args
        self.grammar_queue: List[Req] = []
        if not self.server_args.skip_tokenizer_init:
            self.grammar_backend = create_grammar_backend(
                self.server_args,
                scheduler.tokenizer,
                scheduler.model_config.vocab_size,
                scheduler.model_config.hf_eos_token_id,
            )
        else:
            self.grammar_backend = None

        self.grammar_sync_group = scheduler.dp_tp_cpu_group
        self.grammar_sync_size = scheduler.dp_tp_group.world_size
        self.grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.first_rank
        self.is_grammar_sync_entry = scheduler.dp_tp_group.is_first_rank

        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL = envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL.get()
        self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS = (
            envs.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS.get()
        )

    def __len__(self):
        return len(self.grammar_queue)

    def clear(self):
        if self.grammar_backend:
            self.grammar_backend.reset()

    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
        return len(self.grammar_queue) > 0

    def abort_requests(self, recv_req: AbortReq):
        for req in self.grammar_queue:
            if recv_req.abort_all or req.rid.startswith(recv_req.rid):
                logger.debug(f"Abort grammar queue request. {req.rid=}")
                if req.grammar:
                    req.grammar.cancel()
                req.set_finish_with_abort("Aborted by AbortReq.")

    def process_req_with_grammar(self, req: Req) -> bool:
        # Init grammar cache for this request
        add_to_grammar_queue = False
        if (
            req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None
            or req.sampling_params.regex is not None
            or req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None
            or req.sampling_params.structural_tag is not None
        ):
            if self.grammar_backend is None:
                error_msg = "Grammar-based generation (json_schema, regex, ebnf, structural_tag) is not supported when the server is launched with --grammar-backend none"
                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)
            else:
                if req.sampling_params.json_schema is not None:
                    key = ("json", req.sampling_params.json_schema)
                elif req.sampling_params.regex is not None:
                    key = ("regex", req.sampling_params.regex)
                elif req.sampling_params.ebnf is not None:
                    key = ("ebnf", req.sampling_params.ebnf)
                elif req.sampling_params.structural_tag:
                    key = ("structural_tag", req.sampling_params.structural_tag)

                value, cache_hit = self.grammar_backend.get_cached_or_future_value(
                    key, req.require_reasoning
                )
                req.grammar = value

                if not cache_hit:
                    req.grammar_key = key
                    add_to_grammar_queue = True
                else:
                    if value is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:  # We hit a cached invalid grammar.
                        error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request with cache hit: {key=}"
                        req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        if add_to_grammar_queue:
            self.grammar_queue.append(req)

        return add_to_grammar_queue

    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> List[Req]:
        """
        Move requests whose grammar objects are ready from grammar_queue to waiting_queue.

        Rank i returns two sets ready_reqs_i, failed_reqs_i
        ready_reqs_all = all_gather(ready_reqs_i)
        failed_reqs_all = all_gather(failed_reqs_i)

        ready_reqs = intersect(ready_reqs_all)
        failed_reqs = union(failed_reqs_all)
        """
        ready_req_idxs: set[int] = set()
        failed_req_idxs: set[int] = set()

        # Poll for ready requests
        start_time = time.perf_counter()
        while time.perf_counter() - start_time < self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL:
            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
                if i in ready_req_idxs:
                    continue

                if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)
                    continue

                assert isinstance(req.grammar, futures.Future), f"{req=}"
                if req.grammar.done():
                    ready_req_idxs.add(i)

            # Sleep a bit to avoid busy waiting
            time.sleep(self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL / 10)

        # Check failed requests
        for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue):
            if i not in ready_req_idxs:
                self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct += 1
                if (
                    self.grammar_queue[i].grammar_wait_ct
                    >= self.SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS
                ):
                    # Timeout after max poll iterations
                    # The actual waiting time is SGLANG_GRAMMAR_MAX_POLL_ITERATIONS * max(SGLANG_GRAMMAR_POLL_INTERVAL, GPU_forward_batch_latency)
                    failed_req_idxs.add(i)

        # Sync ready and failed requests across all ranks
        if self.grammar_sync_size == 1:
            synced_ready_req_idxs = ready_req_idxs
            synced_failed_req_idxs = failed_req_idxs
        else:
            all_gather_output = [None] * self.grammar_sync_size
            torch.distributed.all_gather_object(
                all_gather_output,
                (ready_req_idxs, failed_req_idxs),
                group=self.grammar_sync_group,
            )
            synced_ready_req_idxs = set.intersection(*[x[0] for x in all_gather_output])
            synced_failed_req_idxs = set.union(*[x[1] for x in all_gather_output])

        # Return ready requests
        return_reqs: List[Req] = []
        for i in synced_ready_req_idxs:
            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
            return_reqs.append(req)
            if req.finished() or req.grammar is None:  # It is aborted by AbortReq
                continue

            req.grammar = req.grammar.result()
            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, req.grammar.copy())
            if req.grammar is INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ:
                error_msg = f"Invalid grammar request: {req.grammar_key=}"
                req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        # Return failed requests
        for i in synced_failed_req_idxs:
            req = self.grammar_queue[i]
            return_reqs.append(req)

            req.grammar.cancel()
            self.grammar_backend.set_cache(req.grammar_key, INVALID_GRAMMAR_OBJ)
            error_msg = f"Grammar preprocessing timed out: {req.grammar_key=}"
            req.set_finish_with_abort(error_msg)

        # Remove finished requests from grammar_queue
        self.grammar_queue = [
            req
            for i, req in enumerate(self.grammar_queue)
            if i not in synced_ready_req_idxs and i not in synced_failed_req_idxs
        ]
        return return_reqs

# Copyright 2023-2024 SGLang Team
# Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
# you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
# You may obtain a copy of the License at
#
#     http://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
#
# Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
# distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
# WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
# See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
# limitations under the License.
# ==============================================================================
"""Sampling parameters for text generation."""

import logging
from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Union

# sre_parse is deprecated in Python 3.11+, use re._parser instead
try:
    import re._parser as sre_parse
except ImportError:
    import sre_parse  # Python < 3.11

_SAMPLING_EPS = 1e-6
TOP_K_ALL = 1 << 30

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


class SamplingParams:
    """
    The sampling parameters.

    See docs/backend/sampling_params.md or
    https://docs.sglang.io/backend/sampling_params.html
    for the documentation.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        temperature: float = 1.0,
        top_p: float = 1.0,
        top_k: int = -1,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
        repetition_penalty: float = 1.0,
        min_new_tokens: int = 0,
        n: int = 1,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        ebnf: Optional[str] = None,
        structural_tag: Optional[str] = None,
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        skip_special_tokens: bool = True,
        spaces_between_special_tokens: bool = True,
        no_stop_trim: bool = False,
        custom_params: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
        stream_interval: Optional[int] = None,
        logit_bias: Optional[Dict[str, float]] = None,
        sampling_seed: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> None:
        self.max_new_tokens = max_new_tokens
        self.stop_strs = stop
        if stop_token_ids:
            self.stop_token_ids = set(stop_token_ids)
        else:
            self.stop_token_ids = None
        self.stop_regex_strs = stop_regex
        self.temperature = temperature
        self.top_p = top_p
        self.top_k = top_k
        self.min_p = min_p
        self.frequency_penalty = frequency_penalty
        self.presence_penalty = presence_penalty
        self.repetition_penalty = repetition_penalty
        self.min_new_tokens = min_new_tokens
        self.regex = regex
        self.n = n
        self.json_schema = json_schema
        self.ebnf = ebnf
        self.structural_tag = structural_tag
        self.ignore_eos = ignore_eos
        self.skip_special_tokens = skip_special_tokens
        self.spaces_between_special_tokens = spaces_between_special_tokens
        self.no_stop_trim = no_stop_trim
        self.custom_params = custom_params
        self.stream_interval = stream_interval
        self.logit_bias = logit_bias
        self.sampling_seed = sampling_seed

        # Process some special cases
        if 0 <= self.temperature < _SAMPLING_EPS:
            # top_k = 1 means greedy sampling
            self.temperature = 1.0
            self.top_k = 1
        if self.top_k == -1:
            self.top_k = TOP_K_ALL  # whole vocabulary

    def verify(self, vocab_size):
        if self.temperature < 0.0:
            raise ValueError(
                f"temperature must be non-negative, got {self.temperature}."
            )
        if not 0.0 < self.top_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"top_p must be in (0, 1], got {self.top_p}.")
        if not 0.0 <= self.min_p <= 1.0:
            raise ValueError(f"min_p must be in [0, 1], got {self.min_p}.")
        if self.top_k < 1 or self.top_k == -1:
            raise ValueError(
                f"top_k must be -1 (disable) or at least 1, got {self.top_k}."
            )
        if not -2.0 <= self.frequency_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "frequency_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got "
                f"{self.frequency_penalty}."
            )
        if not -2.0 <= self.presence_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "presence_penalty must be in [-2, 2], got " f"{self.presence_penalty}."
            )
        if not 0.0 <= self.repetition_penalty <= 2.0:
            raise ValueError(
                "repetition_penalty must be in [0, 2], got "
                f"{self.repetition_penalty}."
            )
        if not 0 <= self.min_new_tokens:
            raise ValueError(
                f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens], got "
                f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
            )
        if self.max_new_tokens is not None:
            if self.max_new_tokens < 0:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"max_new_tokens must be at least 0, got {self.max_new_tokens}."
                )
            if not self.min_new_tokens <= self.max_new_tokens:
                raise ValueError(
                    f"min_new_tokens must be in [0, max_new_tokens({self.max_new_tokens})], got "
                    f"{self.min_new_tokens}."
                )
        if self.logit_bias is not None:
            for token_id in self.logit_bias:
                if not 0 <= int(token_id) < vocab_size:
                    raise ValueError(
                        f"logit_bias must has keys in [0, {vocab_size - 1}], got "
                        f"{token_id}."
                    )

        grammars = [
            self.json_schema,
            self.regex,
            self.ebnf,
        ]  # since mutually exclusive, only one can be set
        if sum(x is not None for x in grammars) > 1:
            raise ValueError("Only one of regex, json_schema, or ebnf can be set.")

    def normalize(self, tokenizer):
        # Process stop strings
        if self.stop_strs is None:
            self.stop_strs = []
            self.stop_str_max_len = 0
        else:
            if isinstance(self.stop_strs, str):
                self.stop_strs = [self.stop_strs]

            stop_str_max_len = 0
            for stop_str in self.stop_strs:
                if tokenizer is not None:
                    stop_str_ids = tokenizer.encode(stop_str, add_special_tokens=False)
                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str_ids))
                else:
                    stop_str_max_len = max(stop_str_max_len, len(stop_str))
            self.stop_str_max_len = stop_str_max_len

        # Process stop regex strings
        if self.stop_regex_strs is None:
            self.stop_regex_strs = []
            self.stop_regex_max_len = 0
        else:
            if isinstance(self.stop_regex_strs, str):
                self.stop_regex_strs = [self.stop_regex_strs]

            stop_regex_max_len = 0
            for stop_regex in self.stop_regex_strs:
                stop_regex_max_len = max(
                    stop_regex_max_len, get_max_seq_length(stop_regex)
                )

            self.stop_regex_max_len = stop_regex_max_len


# This function gets a strict upperbound on the maximum number of tokens that would need
# to be buffered to match the input regex string
# NOTE: in the worst case, one character that needs to be buffered corresponds to one
# token
def get_max_seq_length(regex_str: str):
    return _max_length_from_subpattern(sre_parse.parse(regex_str))


MAX_LEN = 2**30


def _max_length_from_subpattern(subpattern: sre_parse.SubPattern):
    total = 0
    for token, value in subpattern:
        if token in {
            sre_parse.LITERAL,  # `value` is any one character
            sre_parse.IN,  # Any character within `value`
            sre_parse.ANY,  # "."
        }:
            total += 1
        elif token == sre_parse.SUBPATTERN:
            # EG: (a\d+) ->
            # [(SUBPATTERN,
            #   (1, 0, 0, [(LITERAL, 97),
            #              (MAX_REPEAT, (1, MAXREPEAT, [(IN, [(CATEGORY, CATEGORY_DIGIT)])]))]))]
            _, _, _, inner_subpattern = value
            total += _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
        elif token == sre_parse.BRANCH:
            _, branches = value
            total += max(_max_length_from_subpattern(branch) for branch in branches)
        elif token in {sre_parse.MAX_REPEAT, sre_parse.MIN_REPEAT}:
            _, max_num_repeat, inner_subpattern = value
            if max_num_repeat == sre_parse.MAXREPEAT:
                total += MAX_LEN
            else:
                total += max_num_repeat * _max_length_from_subpattern(inner_subpattern)
        elif token == sre_parse.AT:
            # These are zero-width assertions like ^, $, and \b that don't add to the max
            # length
            total += 0
        else:
            logger.warning(f"Got unhandled regex token: {token}")

            total += MAX_LEN

    return total

"""The intermediate representation."""

import dataclasses
import inspect
import warnings
from typing import List, Optional, Union

from sglang.global_config import global_config
from sglang.lang.choices import ChoicesSamplingMethod

REGEX_INT = r"[-+]?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
REGEX_FLOAT = r"[-+]?[0-9]*\.?[0-9]+[ \n]*"
REGEX_BOOL = r"(True|False)"
REGEX_STR = r"\"[\w\d\s]*\""  # bugs with regex r"\".*\"" in interegular pkg


@dataclasses.dataclass
class SglSamplingParams:
    max_new_tokens: int = 128
    min_new_tokens: int = 0
    n: int = 1
    stop: Union[str, List[str]] = ()
    stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = ()
    stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = ()
    temperature: float = 1.0
    top_p: float = 1.0
    top_k: int = -1  # -1 means disable
    min_p: float = 0.0
    frequency_penalty: float = 0.0
    presence_penalty: float = 0.0
    ignore_eos: bool = False
    return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None
    logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = (None,)
    top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = (None,)
    return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = (None,)
    json_schema: Optional[str] = None

    # for constrained generation, not included in to_xxx_kwargs
    dtype: Optional[str] = None
    regex: Optional[str] = None

    def clone(self):
        return SglSamplingParams(
            self.max_new_tokens,
            self.min_new_tokens,
            self.n,
            self.stop,
            self.stop_token_ids,
            self.stop_regex,
            self.temperature,
            self.top_p,
            self.top_k,
            self.min_p,
            self.frequency_penalty,
            self.presence_penalty,
            self.ignore_eos,
            self.return_logprob,
            self.logprob_start_len,
            self.top_logprobs_num,
            self.return_text_in_logprobs,
            self.json_schema,
        )

    def to_openai_kwargs(self):
        # OpenAI does not support top_k, so we drop it here
        if self.regex is not None:
            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the OpenAI backend.")
        return {
            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
            "max_completion_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
            "n": self.n,
            "stop": self.stop or None,
            "temperature": self.temperature,
            "top_p": self.top_p,
            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
        }

    def to_vertexai_kwargs(self):
        if self.regex is not None:
            warnings.warn(
                "Regular expression is not supported in the VertexAI backend."
            )
        return {
            "candidate_count": 1,
            "max_output_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
            "stop_sequences": self.stop,
            "temperature": self.temperature,
            "top_p": self.top_p,
            "top_k": self.top_k if self.top_k > 0 else None,
        }

    def to_anthropic_kwargs(self):
        # Anthropic does not support frequency_penalty or presence_penalty, so we drop it here
        if self.regex is not None:
            warnings.warn(
                "Regular expression is not supported in the Anthropic backend."
            )
        return {
            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
            "stop_sequences": (
                self.stop if isinstance(self.stop, (list, tuple)) else [self.stop]
            ),
            "temperature": self.temperature,
            "top_p": self.top_p,
            "top_k": self.top_k,
        }

    def to_litellm_kwargs(self):
        if self.regex is not None:
            warnings.warn("Regular expression is not supported in the LiteLLM backend.")
        return {
            "max_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
            "stop": self.stop or None,
            "temperature": self.temperature,
            "top_p": self.top_p,
            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
        }

    def to_srt_kwargs(self):
        return {
            "max_new_tokens": self.max_new_tokens,
            "min_new_tokens": self.min_new_tokens,
            "n": self.n,
            "stop": self.stop,
            "stop_token_ids": self.stop_token_ids,
            "stop_regex": self.stop_regex,
            "temperature": self.temperature,
            "top_p": self.top_p,
            "top_k": self.top_k,
            "min_p": self.min_p,
            "frequency_penalty": self.frequency_penalty,
            "presence_penalty": self.presence_penalty,
            "ignore_eos": self.ignore_eos,
            "regex": self.regex,
            "json_schema": self.json_schema,
        }


class SglFunction:
    def __init__(self, func, num_api_spec_tokens=None, bind_arguments=None):
        self.func = func
        self.num_api_spec_tokens = num_api_spec_tokens
        self.bind_arguments = bind_arguments or {}
        self.pin_prefix_rid = None

        # Parse arguments
        argspec = inspect.getfullargspec(func)
        assert argspec.args[0] == "s", 'The first argument must be "s"'
        self.arg_names = argspec.args[1:]
        self.arg_defaults = argspec.defaults if argspec.defaults is not None else []

    def bind(self, **kwargs):
        assert all(key in self.arg_names for key in kwargs)

        new_bind_dict = {**self.bind_arguments, **kwargs}
        return SglFunction(self.func, bind_arguments=new_bind_dict)

    def run(
        self,
        *args,
        max_new_tokens: int = 128,
        n: int = 1,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        temperature: float = 1.0,
        top_p: float = 1.0,
        top_k: int = -1,
        min_p: float = 0.0,
        frequency_penalty: float = 0.0,
        presence_penalty: float = 0.0,
        ignore_eos: bool = False,
        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
        stream: bool = False,
        backend=None,
        use_thread: bool = True,
        **kwargs,
    ):
        from sglang.lang.interpreter import run_program

        # avoid using [] as the default arg: https://nikos7am.com/posts/mutable-default-arguments/
        if stop is None:
            stop = []
        if stop_token_ids is None:
            stop_token_ids = []
        if stop_regex is None:
            stop_regex = []

        default_sampling_para = SglSamplingParams(
            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
            n=n,
            stop=stop,
            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
            stop_regex=stop_regex,
            temperature=temperature,

54	    backend = backend or global_config.default_backend

56	        return False

139	    )

class SglGen(SglExpr):
    def __init__(
        self,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        max_new_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        min_new_tokens: Optional[int] = None,
        n: Optional[int] = None,
        stop: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        stop_token_ids: Optional[List[int]] = None,
        stop_regex: Optional[Union[str, List[str]]] = None,
        temperature: Optional[float] = None,
        top_p: Optional[float] = None,
        top_k: Optional[int] = None,
        min_p: Optional[float] = None,
        frequency_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        presence_penalty: Optional[float] = None,
        ignore_eos: Optional[bool] = None,
        return_logprob: Optional[bool] = None,
        logprob_start_len: Optional[int] = None,
        top_logprobs_num: Optional[int] = None,
        return_text_in_logprobs: Optional[bool] = None,
        dtype: Optional[type] = None,
        regex: Optional[str] = None,
        json_schema: Optional[str] = None,
    ):
        """Call the model to generate. See the meaning of the arguments in docs/backend/sampling_params.md"""
        super().__init__()
        self.name = name
        self.sampling_params = SglSamplingParams(
            max_new_tokens=max_new_tokens,
            min_new_tokens=min_new_tokens,
            n=n,
            stop=stop,
            stop_regex=stop_regex,
            stop_token_ids=stop_token_ids,
            temperature=temperature,
            top_p=top_p,
            top_k=top_k,
            min_p=min_p,
            frequency_penalty=frequency_penalty,
            presence_penalty=presence_penalty,
            ignore_eos=ignore_eos,
            return_logprob=return_logprob,
            logprob_start_len=logprob_start_len,
            top_logprobs_num=top_logprobs_num,
            return_text_in_logprobs=return_text_in_logprobs,
            dtype=dtype,
            regex=regex,
            json_schema=json_schema,
        )

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"Gen('{self.name}')"


class SglConstantText(SglExpr):
    def __init__(self, value: str):
        super().__init__()
        self.value = value

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"Constant({repr(self.value)})"


class SglRoleBegin(SglExpr):
    def __init__(self, role: str):
        super().__init__()
        self.role = role

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"RoleBegin({self.role})"


class SglRoleEnd(SglExpr):
    def __init__(self, role: str):
        super().__init__()
        self.role = role

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"RoleEnd({self.role})"


class SglSelect(SglExpr):

    def __init__(
        self,
        name: str,
        choices: List[str],
        temperature: float,
        choices_method: ChoicesSamplingMethod,
    ):
        super().__init__()
        self.name = name
        self.choices = choices
        self.temperature = temperature
        self.choices_method = choices_method

    def __repr__(self):
        return f"Select({self.name}, choices={self.choices}, choices_method={self.choices_method})"

    # For PD disaggregation
    bootstrap_host: Optional[Union[List[str], str]] = None
